⚡ Get Showing-Ready
Enter the home details — get your ShowingIQ brief instantly
Instant · AI-powered · Atlanta-specific showing patterns
💬 Buyer Signals
8 Buyer Signals
Older homes
Plumbing — What You're Seeing
"What kind of pipes are these? Are they okay?"
"Those are galvanized — we'd want the inspector to assess where they are in their life cycle."
Any age
Structure — What You're Seeing
"Should I be worried about these cracks?"
"Small hairline cracks are usually normal settling — horizontal ones are the ones worth having the inspector look at."
15+ years
HVAC — What You're Seeing
"Is this a good HVAC system? How old is it?"
"The age is usually on a sticker on the unit — systems typically last 10 to 15 years, so that's always something we look at."
Flag early
Plumbing — Risk Signal
"What are those gray plastic pipes?"
"Those may be polybutylene — we'd want to flag that early because it affects insurability. The inspector will confirm."
Any age
Roof — What You're Seeing
"How old is this roof? Do we need a new one?"
"Roofs typically last 15 to 25 years depending on material — the inspector will assess condition and remaining life."
Atlanta
Structure — Risk Signal
"What's under the house? Should we be worried?"
"The inspector will go under and check — moisture and pests are the main things that matter in Atlanta crawl spaces."
1965–1973
Electrical — What You're Seeing
"I heard aluminum wiring is dangerous — is this house safe?"
"It can be safe with the right setup — we'd want an electrician to confirm it's properly maintained."
Atlanta
Local Patterns — Atlanta Priority
"Are termites really that common in Atlanta?"
"Very common — the key question is whether there's a current termite bond. We always ask for the WIIR in Atlanta transactions."
⚠️ Risk Signals
See all Local Patterns →
🐛
Termites + WIIR
Required at every Georgia transfer
💧
Crawl space moisture
Humidity + pests + rot
🔴
Mold indicators
24–48 hr window after water damage
🧱
EIFS / stucco
Water trap — specialist always
🏗️
Soil movement
What Atlanta clay actually does
🌊
Flood zones
FEMA · insurance · 2009 context
☢️
Radon
4 pCi/L action level · test every home
🌳
Trees + sewers
Root intrusion · Intown risk
🔨
Flip quality
What's cosmetic vs what's hidden
🌿
Stream buffers
DeKalb 75 ft · Fulton 50–75 ft · affects buildable land
🔍 What You're Seeing
See all →
🚿
Plumbing
6 entries
Copper vs PEX vs galvanized
PVC (drains) vs CPVC (supply)
Polybutylene — Cox v. Shell (1995)
Water heater age + type
⚡
Electrical
3 entries
Breaker panel vs fuse box
Aluminum wiring — connection risk
Knob-and-tube — insurance flag
🌡️
HVAC
2 entries
Age and type — heat pump vs furnace
Ductwork in Atlanta humidity
2–4× energy efficiency (DOE)
🏠
Structure + Exterior
4 entries
Roof — flashing drives most findings
Foundation cracks — reading them right
EIFS — specialist evaluation always
⚖️ Disclosure and Deal Risk
High ShowingIQ miss
Lead paint — pre-1978 homes
Federal affirmative obligation on sellers and agents. Not optional.
→
WDO / WIIR — Georgia termite report
Required at every Georgia property transfer. Know the name.
→
Unpermitted additions
Affects insurance, financing, and resale. Check county permit records.
→
Polybutylene pipes — insurability
Class action settlement 1995. Most insurers require full replacement.
→
Radon — 4 pCi/L action level
EPA recommends testing all homes regardless of zone.
→
Stream buffers — Fulton & DeKalb
DeKalb: 75 ft. Fulton: 50–75 ft + 25 ft impervious setback. Can make land unbuildable.
→
31
ShowingIQ topics

Recently Viewed
The Rule That Protects You
Never say
"This is fine."
"This is a problem."
"You'll need to replace this."
Always say
"We'd want the inspector to confirm that."
"Good thing to flag for the inspector."
"Let's make note of that."
